sample_stories.title: [Reservation Svc] Feature-flag scaffolding & kill-switch

narrative.i_want: feature flags and a kill-switch wired into Reservation Svc before any functional work
narrative.so_that: we can safely ramp and instantly rollback during pilot and GA

acceptance_criteria: LaunchDarkly client integrated in Reservation Svc
Flag `bopis_v2_enabled` gates the POST /reservations endpoint end-to-end
Kill-switch flag `bopis_v2_kill` short-circuits writes within ≤ 30s of flip
Flag states visible in `bopis-v2-health` dashboard
dod: Integration test: flip kill-switch under load, verify < 30s effect
Runbook entry: how to flip kill-switch

sample_stories.title: [Reservation Svc] Schema migration expand — reservations + pickup_events tables

narrative.i_want: new tables created via an additive migration
narrative.so_that: downstream work can write against the real schema without risk to existing paths

acceptance_criteria: Aurora migration adds `reservation` and `pickup_event` tables per data-model.v1.md
Migration is backward-compatible (no existing reads/writes affected)
Indexes on (store_id, expires_at), (reservation_id, event_type)
Rollback script verified in stage

sample_stories.title: [Reservation Svc] Atomic decrement + 90-min hold (happy path)

narrative.i_want: my reservation to hold inventory for 90 minutes exactly
narrative.so_that: the store has my item when I arrive and it's not double-sold

acceptance_criteria: POST /reservations with Idempotency-Key atomically decrements inventory via Redis WATCH/MULTI
Inserts reservation row in Aurora with expires_at = now + 90 min
Returns 201 with reservation_id within p95 ≤ 300ms
Publishes `reservation.created` on Kafka with trace-id
Idempotent: identical Idempotency-Key returns prior result without double-writing
dod: Integration tests against stage Redis + Aurora
Runbook: how to manually release a stuck reservation

sample_stories.title: [Reservation Svc] Concurrent last-unit: prevent oversell

narrative.i_want: exactly one customer to win the last unit
narrative.so_that: we never oversell and never disappoint a customer on arrival

acceptance_criteria: Given inventory = 1 and two concurrent reservation attempts, exactly one succeeds
Loser receives 409 with `alt_stores[]` payload (up to 3 within 25 mi)
Inventory count is never negative (audit check emits alert on divergence)
Stress test: 1000 parallel attempts on qty=5 → exactly 5 wins, 995 losses, 0 oversells
dod: Unit tests on WATCH/MULTI retry path

sample_stories.title: [Inventory Read Svc] Shadow mode — log OMS vs cache divergence for 1 week pre-pilot

narrative.i_want: a shadow-mode comparison of cached vs live OMS inventory
narrative.so_that: we validate our 30s staleness assumption on real traffic before any customer sees a cache result

acceptance_criteria: Shadow mode flag `inv_read_shadow` samples 5% of prod traffic
For each sampled request: return live OMS result to user AND log `cached_qty` vs `live_qty` with timestamps
Divergence report generated daily; human review before enabling read-from-cache
No customer impact in shadow mode
dod: 1-week shadow run signed off by PM + Ops before enabling read-from-cache

sample_stories.title: [Associate App] Arrival notification via Kafka consumer

narrative.i_want: an in-app 'customer arrived' notification within 5 seconds of the kiosk scan
narrative.so_that: I can greet the customer and hand off promptly

acceptance_criteria: Consume `pickup.arrived` from Kafka
Route to on-shift associates at the relevant store via Notifications Platform push
Push latency p95 ≤ 5s from event to device
Respects quiet-hours and associate role filters
Section 508 accessibility validated on the Associate App screen

sample_stories.title: [QE] Peak load test — 12K RPS inventory read for 60 min

narrative.i_want: a reproducible load-test harness meeting PRD NFR targets
narrative.so_that: we enter peak season with documented headroom
narrative.note: Also validates AC-009 performance acceptance criterion

acceptance_criteria: k6 harness runs 12K RPS sustained 60 min against stage
p95 ≤ 400ms, error rate ≤ 0.1%
Cache hit ratio ≥ 95%
No cache stampede observed; coalescing effectiveness measured
Report archived with pass/fail per NFR
dod: Report signed off by SRE + arch

sample_stories.title: [QE] Chaos test — OMS outage + Payments 5xx

narrative.i_want: verified graceful degradation when OMS or Payments fails
narrative.so_that: customer browse continues and we honor AC-003

acceptance_criteria: Toxiproxy/chaos script kills OMS for 5 min in stage under load
Browse path never returns customer-visible error
Circuit breaker opens within 10s; fallback UI renders
Payments 5xx scenario: retry once then 'try another method' message
Error-budget burn alerts fire as expected

sample_stories.title: [QE] WCAG 2.2 AA audit + fix cycle on customer-facing surfaces

narrative.i_want: automated and manual WCAG 2.2 AA verification before GA
narrative.so_that: we comply with AC-008 and do not ship a barrier to AT users

acceptance_criteria: Axe automated in CI on PDP inventory module, cart, reservation confirmation
Manual VoiceOver + TalkBack verification logged
All critical + major findings fixed; minor tracked
Sign-off from accessibility lead

sample_stories.title: [Ops] Runbook + on-call rotation + SLO dashboard

narrative.i_want: a BOPIS v2 runbook, dashboards, and rotation before pilot
narrative.so_that: incidents are diagnosable and actionable on night 1

acceptance_criteria: `bopis-v2-health` dashboard live (RED metrics + business events + SLO burn-rate)
Alerts: OMS breaker open, oversell divergence, SLO burn > 2× budget, DLQ > 10
Runbook covers: kill-switch, force-release reservation, replay DLQ, failover to us-west-2
On-call rotation assigned for first 4 weeks post-pilot
Drill: kill-switch + failover exercised pre-pilot

sample_stories.title: [Rollout] Associate training e-learning + launch-day huddle deck

narrative.i_want: pilot-store associates trained on the new flow before go-live
narrative.so_that: the first real customer pickup is smooth

acceptance_criteria: 30-min e-learning module shipped via LMS; completion tracked per associate
Huddle deck (5 min) for launch-day morning meeting
100% completion in 5 pilot stores before pilot start
FAQ + escalation card posted at service desk

note: This JSON shows 11 representative Stories out of 47. Full decomposition lives in this file in a real run.
